level_signals: Base range $182,308 - $260,441 consistent with principal-level IC at a large tech company
Expected to set standards across the org and influence senior leadership

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of product management experience, with a majority in AI, consumer-focused roles
Proven track record shipping and scaling agentic products in production at scale — real agents that take autonomous action for users, not prototypes, demos, or scripted chatbots
Experience owning consumer products with tens or hundreds of millions of users
Demonstrated ability to balance investment across maturing and emerging technologies — knowing when to bet on the new and when to lean on the proven
Demonstrated ability to set product vision and roadmap and bring executive and developer-leadership audiences along
Exceptional product taste and instinct for what makes an experience delightful
Decisiveness under incomplete information: you triangulate the best evidence available, make the call, and own the consequences — "the AI recommended it" is never an excuse
Highly analytical; fluent in experimentation and using data to drive decisions
Excellent communication and stakeholder management across large, distributed, cross-functional orgs
Deep, current expertise in how AI and agents are transforming consumer software, with a track record of building and launching agentic capabilities at scale in production
Experience with recommender systems, personalization, or large-scale ML-driven products — and the judgment to decide where an established stack stays durable versus where to move onto a new paradigm

Technical background or proven ability to partner closely with ML engineers and researchers on what's feasible — comfortable pulling your own data, exploring a codebase, or spinning up a prototype to unblock yourself
Experience building evaluation frameworks for AI-driven or personalized products at scale
Experience influencing across broad organizations and helping teams adopt new ways of working
You have either founder or startup experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Passion for audio, discovery, and redefining how the world finds what it loves next

explicitly_excluded: prototypes, demos, or scripted chatbots (not acceptable as evidence of agentic product experience)

scope.surface: Home (primary), with mandate extending to Now Playing, Browse, and other surfaces as strategy matures
primary_metrics_inferred: User engagement and retention on Home surface
Discovery quality (content listening attributed to Home recommendations)
A/B experiment win rate on agentic experiences
core_responsibilities: Own the product vision, strategy, and roadmap for the agentic future of Home, with flexibility to extend across Now Playing, Browse, and other surfaces as the strategy matures

Set the strategy for how Home's personalization evolves — determining where the established recommendation stack remains durable and where we deprecate it in favor of agents and the Large Personalization Model
Lead the ideation, development, and launch of agentic experiences that move from reactive recommendations to proactive, intent-aware action
Translate frontier model and agent capabilities into intuitive experiences a broad global audience understands and loves
Establish the evaluation bar for agentic experiences: rigorous eval frameworks, instrumentation, and honest assessment across diverse user segments
Use prototypes and real results to learn — favor showing a working prototype over writing a long doc
Use a mix of quantitative data, experimentation, and user research to understand behavior and make prioritization calls
Partner deeply with engineering, ML, design, research, and data science to ship high-impact improvements at a fast cadence
Influence senior stakeholders across Spotify, building alignment around a vision that spans multiple teams and surfaces

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($182,308 - $260,441 base) plus equity
Clear product surface ownership (Home, largest Spotify surface)
Explicit mandate scope with potential to expand across surfaces
Fully remote within North America — no office requirement stated
Generous benefits package itemized (6-month parental leave, 23 days PTO, 13 flexible holidays)
Role described as 'defining' — high visibility and strategic importance
red_flags: Extremely rare requirement: production agentic products at 'tens or hundreds of millions' of users scale — very few candidates globally qualify
Founder or startup experience listed as a plain unconditional bullet (treated as hard requirement here but may be aspirational — ambiguous)
High buzzword density throughout JD — 'agentic', 'AI-native', 'Large Personalization Model' repeated without precise definitions
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned — unclear if Spotify sponsors for this role
Eastern Standard time zone collaboration requirement may limit candidates in Pacific time zones despite 'North America' framing

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 'Principal' title, 10+ years requirement, and comp range)
scope.business_model (inferred from Spotify's known consumer subscription/ad model)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not stated in JD; inferred from responsibilities)
location.work_auth (not stated; left as no-sponsorship because visa sponsorship is not mentioned — flagged as ambiguous)
ambiguities: 'You have either founder or startup experience' appears as a plain unconditional bullet in 'Who You Are' — treated as hard must-have per schema rules, but it may function as a strong preference rather than a firm gate. Worth confirming with recruiter.

Location says 'North America region as long as we have a work location' — interpreted as remote-us eligible but could include Canada; treated as remote-us given compensation is stated in USD and the US range is explicitly noted.
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD; work_auth set to no-sponsorship and visa_sponsorship to unclear as a conservative default.
